Title: OPR status quo throughout 2026
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia is not expected to increase or reduce its overnight policy rate at 2.75 per cent throughout the year, despite higher energy-related inflation risks.
Kenanga Investment Bank Bhd (Kenanga IB) said a hike is unlikely as inflation remains largely cost‑driven and domestic growth still faces external headwinds.
The central bank, Kenanga IB said, is expected to prioritise growth support over pre‑emptive tightening.
"A stable policy rate should continue to provide certainty for households and businesses, limiting credit stress and supporting borrowing activity," the firm noted in a report.
Further policy support is more likely to come via targeted fiscal and financial measures, including an additional RM5.0 billion in micro, small and medium enterprise financing under Bank Negara and a RM5.0 billion expansion in Syarikat Jaminan Pembiayaan Perniagaan guarantees,
Both schemes should help sustain SME credit access, particularly in energy‑sensitive sectors, and cushion downside risks to business loan growth.
In another report, Kenanga IB said the US Federal Reserve's (Fed) inflation-focused stance had raised the bar for near-term easing, turning attention to whether upcoming US labour and inflation data can materially soften policy expectations.
It noted that he incoming US payrolls, wage growth, European Central Bank, Bank of England and Bank Negara decisions will be key in shaping rate differentials, inflation expectations, and regional currency sentiment.
Overall, Kenanga IB said its baseline still assumes eventual geopolitical deescalation in the third quarter  2026, which should reverse much of crude oil's war premium over time.
However, the firm said prolonged energy volatility and the Fed's more hawkish posture suggest tighter financial conditions may persist longer.
"We therefore maintain a cautious US dollar/ringgit range of 3.94-3.98 next week, with upside risks skewed toward the upper band should Hormuz tensions escalate further," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rafizi to be questioned by MACC over ARM deal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli said he has been summoned by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission for questioning over the government's joint venture with chip design firm Arm Holdings.
In a statement today, Rafizi said he received a notice at 3pm to appear at the MACC headquarters in Putrajaya on Monday at 10am.
He said the investigation centres on allegations that he had pressured the Cabinet to expedite approval of the deal, which critics claim was rushed and detrimental to the country.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Commando's family demands findings of army probe into alleged assault
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The family of commando Abdul Hamid Talib is demanding that the findings of the army's internal investigation into an alleged assault that left him severely injured be disclosed.
Suzana Norlihan Alias, the lawyer representing the family, said they have yet to be informed of the results despite public statements on the matter.
She said the family is seeking full details, including the identity of the investigators and the specific findings.
"We have not been officially notified of the investigation results and wish to know how the probe was conducted and who was involved.
"The family hopes for a comprehensive explanation to ensure transparency in this case," she said when contacted.
Yesterday, Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the Malaysian Army had completed its probe into the incident.
He said the findings would be submitted soon and that action against those involved would be determined based on the results.
Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad confirmed last night that two 24-year-old soldiers from the same camp were detained following a police report lodged by the family on March 14.
He said police seized a cane believed to have been used in the incident.
Suzana said the family wants those allegedly involved to face "serious" charges, given Abdul Hamid's severe injuries.
"They want justice for him," she said.
The case is being investigated under Section 326 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing grievous hurt by dangerous weapons, which carries a jail term of up to 20 years, a fine, or whipping.
Suzana said the family has requested that all matters related to the victim be channelled through his legal counsel.
She said Abdul Hamid remains weak and is currently unfit for visitors.
"Doctors have advised that visits be postponed for now as the victim's condition is still unstable.
"The family also wants to focus fully on his care," she said.
It was previously reported that Abdul Hamid was severely injured at an army camp in Johor on March 12.
The 25-year-old from Semporna, Sabah, is attached to the 22nd Commando Regiment at Kem Iskandar.
He is currently unable to speak, relies on a ventilator, and receives nutrition through a feeding tube following serious head injuries.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain forecast across almost all states until 6pm: MetMalaysia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR:  Nearly all states nationwide are forecast to experience thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds until 6pm today.
The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) issued an alert at 3.50pm, stating that the affected states in the northern region are Perlis and Kedah, covering the districts of Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Yan, Pendang and Sik.
In Perak, the areas involved are Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim.
Similar conditions are also forecast for several areas on the east coast, in Kelantan (Jeli, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang), Terengganu (Besut, Setiu, Hulu Terengganu, Marang, Dungun and Kemaman), as well as Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Jerantut, Bera and Rompin).
The same warning has also been issued for Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak and Petaling in Selangor, as well as the entire Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur.
In the southern region, the warning involves Negeri Sembilan (Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah, Rembau, Jempol and Tampin), the entire state of Melaka, and Johor (Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Batu Pahat, Kluang, Pontian, Kulai and Johor Bahru).
Meanwhile, in Sabah, the forecast covers the Interior (Sipitang, Tenom and Nabawan), West Coast (Papar), and Tawau (Kunak, Semporna, and Lahad Datu).
In Sarawak, the affected areas are Kuching (Bau and Lundu), Sarikei (Julau and Meradong), Sibu, Mukah Kapit (Song), Bintulu (Bintulu and Sebauh), Miri (Subis, Beluru, Telang Usan and Marudi) and Limbang.– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Activists on Gaza aid flotilla detained by Israel disembark in Crete
Article:
ATHENS: Dozens of activists on a Gaza-bound aid flotilla which was intercepted by Israeli forces in international waters off Crete disembarked on Friday in the Greek island, an AFP journalist saw.
Escorted by Greek coastguards, around 175 activists, the majority of them nationals of European countries, were taken in four coaches to the port of Atherinolakkos, in the southeast of the island.
As they approached the port, the activists chanted "Free Palestine," AFP saw.
Israel's foreign ministry earlier said around 175 activists had been taken off more than 20 boats on Thursday. Flotilla organisers put the number at 211.
The flotilla comprising more than 50 vessels set sail in recent weeks from Marseille in France, Barcelona in Spain and Syracuse in Italy.
Its aim, according to the organisers, was to break the blockade of Gaza and bring humanitarian aid to the Palestinian territory.
Israel controls all entry points to Gaza. It has been accused by the United Nations and foreign NGOs of strangling the flow of goods into the territory, causing shortages since the start of Israel's war against the Palestinian militant group Hamas in October 2023.
The Gaza Strip, governed by Hamas, has been under an Israeli blockade since 2007.
Israeli foreign ministry spokesman Oren Marmorstein on Friday said: "All the flotilla activists are now in Greece except Saif Abu Keshek and Thiago Avila."
In a post on X, the ministry called the flotilla "another provocation designed to divert attention from Hamas's refusal to disarm." It also said the flotilla was serving "professional provocateurs."
It said Keshek was "suspected of affiliation with a terrorist organisation" and that he and Avila, suspected of "illegal activity", would be brought to Israel for questioning.
Several European governments with nationals among those arrested have called on Israel to free the activists and called its action a flagrant contravention of international law.
But the United States backed Israeli authorities, calling the flotilla a "stunt" and saying it expects allies to deny port access, docking, departure and refueling to vessels participating in the flotilla.
A State Department spokesman said Washington was exploring imposing "consequences" on those who support the flotilla.
The war in Gaza, triggered by the Hamas attacks on Israel on Oct 7, 2023, has led to severe shortages of food, water, medicine and fuel.
In the summer and autumn of 2025, a first voyage by the Global Sumud Flotilla across the Mediterranean towards Gaza drew worldwide attention.
The boats in that flotilla were intercepted by Israel off the coasts of Egypt and the Gaza Strip in early October.
Crew members, including Swedish activist Greta Thunberg, were arrested and then expelled by Israel.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Heavy rain helps extinguish week-long peat fire in Dungun
Article:
DUNGUN: Several bouts of heavy rain in the district brought relief to firefighters, leading to the full closure of a peat forest fire operation at Kampung Baru Kuala Abang, near here, this afternoon.
Dungun Fire and Rescue Station chief Deputy Fire Superintendent II Yahaya Hanafi, said 3 heavy rain spells from 7am helped extinguish the remaining 1.5ha still burning.
He said the prolonged hot and dry weather without rainfall had caused a 34-hectare peat forest area at the site to catch fire since April 24.
"Alhamdulillah, on this blessed Friday morning, God has answered our prayers, including those of the villagers, and we were able to close the operation here at about 3pm today.
"Since the fire began, a firefighting team of 90 personnel worked hard, remaining on site throughout," he said after closing the operations room here this afternoon.
Hanafi said the peat forest fire had forced the suspension of leave for all personnel.
"The fire here was very challenging as it spread towards a nearby settlement of 500 residents.
"This meant our personnel had to remain on alert at all times to prevent the fire from spreading to homes," he said.
He added that thick smoke from the fire also blanketed nearby residential areas and two schools.
"Fortunately, residents and elderly patients were able to cooperate by relocating temporarily when instructed by the authorities.
"Continuous efforts by the firefighting team also ensured that more than 1,000 students from both nearby schools could resume schooling as usual," he said.
Hanafi said that during the peat fire operation, his team also had to respond to fires involving chalets, forest and bush areas, as well as spend two days in a search operation for a man who was lost in the forest.
"We would like to thank everyone who assisted, especially villagers, the police, the Civil Defence Force, Rela, Risda, the Rantau Abang state constituency office and the Dungun parliamentary office," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Foreign woman dies in suspected fall from apartment in Nilai
Article:
NILAI: A foreign woman in her 20s died after she is believed to have fallen from the 13th floor of an apartment building in Bandar Baru Nilai at 1.51am today.
Negri Sembilan police chief Datuk Ahmad Dzaffir Mohd Yussof said the Nilai district police headquarters received information on the incident while a police team was conducting a narcotics raid at the apartment building.
"During the raid, the woman is believed to have attempted to escape through the balcony of the apartment unit before slipping and falling from the 13th floor," he said in a statement today.
He said a motorcycle patrol unit was dispatched to the location and found the victim unconscious.
"A medical team from the Nilai Health Clinic later pronounced her dead at the scene, and the body was sent to Rembau Hospital for a post-mortem.
"Preliminary investigations at the scene have not revealed any criminal elements so far, and the case has been classified as sudden death. However, further investigations are ongoing, including recording statements from witnesses," he said.
He advised the public not to speculate on the matter, as it could disrupt police investigations, and urged those with information to contact the Nilai district police headquarters or the nearest police station.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AEON to open KL Midtown mall in Q4
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Aeon Co (M) Bhd is set to strengthen its foothold in Malaysia's retail sector with the opening of Aeon Mall KL Midtown, scheduled for the fourth quarter of this year.
The new mall forms part of the 75-acre KL Metropolis mixed development, marking Aeon's strategic expansion into the heart of the capital.
With a net lettable area of 367,000 sq ft, Aeon Mall KL Midtown will be integrated with office towers, luxury residences and the five-star Hyatt Regency Kuala Lumpur.
Managing director Tsugutoshi Seiko said the project reflects Aeon's efforts to evolve its retail approach in line with changing consumer expectations and urban development trends.
"Consumers today are increasingly seeking destinations where they can spend quality time, whether through dining, socialising or leisure.
"Beyond retail, we aim to contribute positively to the surrounding community by creating spaces that support daily needs and encourage interaction," he said in a statement.
He added that the mall represents Aeon's first venture within a large-scale integrated development of this nature, marking a significant step in enhancing value for customers, business partners and the wider community.
Aeon Mall KL Midtown will be the company's 28th mall in Malaysia.
The development is expected to enhance retail offerings within KL Metropolis while contributing to a more diverse commercial and lifestyle landscape in Kuala Lumpur.
An AEON supermarket will serve as the anchor tenant, with additional key tenants to be announced progressively as leasing activities advance.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: GOF seizes frozen food, aquatic animals worth RM23k in Rantau Panjang
Article:
KOTA BARU: The General Operations Force (GOF) seized various types of frozen food, as well as fish, prawns and ornamental turtles suspected to have been smuggled from a neighbouring country, worth RM22,760 in an operation at the illegal Lan Bundle base near Rantau Panjang, Pasir Mas, yesterday.
GOF Southeast Brigade commander Datuk Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the seizure was carried out by the Strike Force/Tiger Platoon of Battalion 9 under Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan at 6.30pm.
He said that during a patrol in Kampung Lubok Gong, personnel spotted a group of men unloading black plastic packages and polystyrene boxes from a boat into a vehicle on the riverbank.
"However, all the individuals fled towards a neighbouring country by boat as soon as they realised the presence of the authorities.
"An inspection of the abandoned Proton Wira found 234kg of frozen food and various aquatic animals believed to have been smuggled through illegal routes," he said in a statement today.
He said among those seized were 120 rasbora, 20 koi, 60 killifish, 45 nilem fish, as well as various species of prawns and 60 turtles.
Ahmad Radzi said no arrests were made and the case has been handed over to the Rantau Panjang Customs Department for further action under Section 135(1)(e) of the Customs Act 1967.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan ruler performs prayers at mosque in Jempol
Article:
JEMPOL: Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, today made his first public appearance following a political crisis in the state when His Royal Highness arrived to perform Friday prayers at the Tuanku Muhriz Mosque in Bandar Seri Jempol.
Accompanying him were his two sons, the Tunku Besar Seri Menanti, Tunku Ali Redhauddin Tuanku Muhriz, and Tunku Zain Al-'Abidin Tuanku Muhriz.
Tuanku Muhriz arrived at 1pm and was received by the Penghulu of Luak Jempol, Datuk Lela Putra Setiawan, Datuk Mazri Mansor, and two other Penghulus of the "Luak Tanah Mengandung" (Inas and Gunung Pasir), as well as other customary chiefs.
Following the prayers, Tuanku Muhriz and his sons distributed donations to children, as a gesture that has become a hallmark of his visits.
It has long been Tuanku Muhriz's practice to perform Friday prayers alongside the people in every "Luak Tanah Mengandung", using the opportunity to engage and mingle with the people.
On Wednesday, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun sought an audience with Tuanku Muhriz following a state leadership crisis.
The meeting follows a shock announcement by all 14
Barisan Nasional (BN) assemblymen
that they had unanimously withdrawn their support for the Aminuddin-led state government.
Five Perikatan Nasional (PN) state assemblymen have also submitted a written offer to Umno and BN to cooperate to ensure the state's political stability is restored.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No official word on abducted GSF Malaysian activists 38 hours on, says command centre
Article:
BANDAR BARU BANGI: The Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) has yet to receive any information on the fate of Malaysians allegedly abducted by Israeli forces while taking part in the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) humanitarian mission.
SNCC director-general Datuk Dr Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby confirmed that 38 hours had passed without any official information reaching the centre.
He urged all parties, particularly Malaysians, to continue praying and to maintain pressure for the release of the 175 activists involved, saying the process was expected to be more challenging this time.
"Up to this 38-hour mark, we have still not received any official information. I ask everyone to continue praying.
"I also urge all parties to continue pressing, and hope Members of Parliament, whether from the government or opposition, will unite and apply pressure on the regime through whatever channels are available.
"They have committed no wrongdoing as they were in international waters," he said at a special press conference here today.
Yesterday, SNCC confirmed the presence of dozens of drones around the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF 2.0), believed to have been conducting surveillance and disrupting communications early yesterday morning.
Following reported aggressive actions and intrusion, 10 Malaysians aboard six flotilla vessels were said to have lost contact.
The incident, which occurred between 1am and 11am Malaysian time, also saw the Israel Defence Forces (IDF) allegedly firing warning shots and carrying out radio frequency jamming against unarmed humanitarian activists located 656 nautical miles from the Gaza coast.
In a related development, Sani Araby said SNCC had received reports that all 175 GSF 2.0 activists were seen at the port of Larapetra in Greece at noon (Malaysian time) today, but stressed that the information could not be verified.
"We are reviewing the information, but the status has not been confirmed through official channels or our ground contacts at the port," he said.
He added that SNCC was prepared for the possibility of the activists being released in Greece.
"We are reviewing several reports and are on standby at the port. We have also been informed by Wisma Putra that consular teams and representatives are preparing in the event all participants are released in Greece.
"However, anything can happen," he said.
Sani Araby described the alleged abduction of GSF 2.0 activists in international waters as a desperate act by Israel.
"If some see this as a repeat script, we believe it is a repeat pattern of Israeli wrongdoing, but this time they appear more desperate.
"Our assumption is that they do not want this group of detainees to join a larger group," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: IOI Properties rides Singapore office rebound
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: IOI Properties Group Bhd is emerging as a key beneficiary of the strengthening Singapore office market, with analysts highlighting its growing exposure to prime assets as a major driver of value creation.
IOI Properties is rapidly establishing itself as a significant commercial landlord in Singapore, following two major acquisitions in recent years - South Beach in late 2025 and Asia Square Tower 2 (AST2).
According to UBS Global Research, IOI Properties is set to deliver sustained net asset value (NAV) growth of more than two per cent per annum (p.a) with the rising Singapore office market.
UBS thinks the recent acquisition of AST2 will consolidate market share of landlords to raise their bargaining power, with IOI Properties effectively being the third largest landlord in Grade A core central business district (CBD) offices in Singapore.
"At around half of total assets and near 66 per cent of IOI Properties' NAV, we expect sustained five to 10 per cent rent reversions on 20- 33 per cent p.a renewals to drive meaningful more than two per cent p.a. accretion to NAV/share beyond 2026.
"Low interest rates, credit access and rising private equity interest should support low three per cent office cap rates," it said.
Meanwhile, DBS Research said upon completion of AST 2 acquisition, IOI Properties will see its Singapore investment property portfolio scale to nearly S$10 billion.
This includes landmark project IOI Central Boulevard Towers, valued at around S$4.2 billion, and mixed-use development South Beach Tower at around S$2.8 billion.
"Collectively, these three assets will account for more than 70 per cent of the company's investment property portfolio, cementing it as one of the largest owners of prime office assets in the CBD.
"From a funding perspective, the strengthening ringgit against the Singapore dollar alongside a low SORA environment could also serve as tailwinds for the group as it looks to diversify its business outside of Malaysia," it said in a separate note.
Overall, DBS Research said beyond immediate income from its deployment to Singapore, IOI Properties' growing footprint there also provides a strategic platform for future capital recycling, partnerships and potential monetisation pathways.
"As the group scales its presence, it enhances its ability to participate in larger transactions, drive operational synergies across assets, and establish itself as a credible long-commercial real estate landscape," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Umno wings criticise order to vacate Undang Luak Sungei Ujong's residence
Article:
SEREMBAN: Negri Sembilan Umno's three wings have spoken out against the state government's purported order for the immediate vacation of the Undang Luak Sungei Ujong official residence and the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong.
The state Wanita, Youth and Puteri wings described the move as rushed.
"It is clear that the order was issued at a time when the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong is managing an unresolved dispute involving the Undang Luak Sungei Ujong.
"Therefore, the state government's action is inappropriate, insensitive and can be seen as an administrative move that pre-empts 'adat' (customary) processes."
The wings said the Undang Luak institution is part of Negri Sembilan's adat structure, which is entrenched in the Negri Sembilan Constitution (Undang-Undang Tubuh Kerajaan Negri Sembilan).
They said any order related to the official residence, balai, staff and privileges linked to the institution must be made with due care and respect.
"We believe this rushed move will only reinforce concerns that this crisis is not being managed well by the menteri besar.
"This is not about party politics but the failure of a menteri besar to handle a crisis respectfully and in accordance with adat."
Yesterday, it was reported that the Negri Sembilan State Secretary's Office issued a letter directing the immediate vacation of the Undang Luak Sungei Ujong official residence and the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong.
The April 29 letter was addressed to the private secretary of Datuk Mubarak Dohak, who was stripped of his position as the Undang Luak Sungei Ujong earlier this month.
The letter cited a decision made during a special session of the Dewan Keadilan dan Undang on April 17.
State Secretary Datuk Mohd Zafir Ibrahim declined to comment when contacted, but did not deny the letter's existence.
On Wednesday, Mubarak and three other Undang held a meeting at the Balai Luak Sungei Ujong amid an ongoing dispute with the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah allocates RM559.85mil to boost food security, agriculture
Article:
PAPAR: The Sabah government has allocated RM559.85 million this year to strengthen food security and improve the state's self-sufficiency rate.
Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the allocation to the Ministry of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industry would support various initiatives, including the development of padi cultivation in districts such as Papar, Membakut, Beaufort and Kuala Penyu.
"In line with the spirit of the Kaamatan Festival, which celebrates the harvest, the agriculture sector remains one of the three main pillars driving the state's economic growth," he said at the Sabah-level Kaamatan Festival launch here today.
Hajiji said the move reflects the state's commitment to strengthening food security amid global economic and geopolitical uncertainties.
He added that unity among Sabah's multi-racial and multi-religious communities remains crucial in ensuring continued development under the Hala Tuju Sabah Maju Jaya 2.0 roadmap.
The government, he said, would also continue rolling out initiatives to improve living standards, including support in education, human capital development and public welfare.
"This is to ensure inclusive and balanced development across the state, so that all segments of society can benefit," he said.
Hajiji said Papar was chosen as the launch venue as it is one of Sabah's key padi-producing areas and reflects the state's agricultural strength and cultural diversity.
He added that ongoing infrastructure and commercial developments, including in Benoni and Kinarut, would further enhance the district's growth prospects.
"With the completion of the Pan Borneo Highway, Papar has the potential to become a key stopover for visitors from Brunei and Sarawak," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Japanese man arrested for allegedly incinerating wife's body at zoo
Article:
TOKYO: Japanese police arrested a man for allegedly incinerating his dead wife at the zoo where he worked, officials and local media said Friday, following the discovery of human remains.
Police arrested Tatsuya Suzuki on Thursday evening on suspicion that he "transported the victim's body to a tourist facility" in the northern island of Hokkaido and "destroyed it through incineration there," a local police official told AFP.
The victim, 33-year-old Yui Suzuki, was identified by local media as his wife. Police have not said how she died.
While held in voluntary police questioning, Suzuki said he used his zoo's incinerator – meant to dispose of waste and dead animals – to burn the woman's body "for a few hours," local media reported.
His confession sent police scouring the incinerator for her remains, whose partial discovery paved the way for Suzuki's arrest, local media said.
While alive, the wife once told her relatives that her husband was threatening to "burn you until no trace of you will be left", public broadcaster NHK said, citing investigative sources.
The gruesome case forced the Asahiyama Zoo, a popular tourist attraction closed since early last month as part of regular maintenance ahead of the summer season, to delay its reopening scheduled for Wednesday.
On Friday, the zoo resumed business, with officials bowing to visitors and apologising for the trouble caused.
"The zoo is in an extremely difficult situation at the moment," Hirosuke Imazu, mayor of Asahikawa City, which operates the facility, said, according to NHK.
"But we would like to turn your support into our energy, and convey the beautiful lives of our animals," he said. --AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Labour Day: Govt rolls out RM710mil Pace package, maternity aid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: In conjunction with Labour Day 2026, themed "Pekerja Madani, Kesuma Bangsa", workers in Malaysia are set to benefit from several initiatives, announcements and opportunities introduced at the national-level celebration.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced a series of measures during the Labour Day 2026 celebration:
1. Economic resilience package (Pace) worth more than RM710 million.
- Pace forms part of the government's economic resilience strategy, with positive implications for human capital development and labour market stability.
- It aims to strengthen the labour market, human capital development and career sustainability.
- Four key initiatives under Pace 2026:
- Social protection readiness.
- Training empowerment and job placement.
- Gig worker empowerment.
- Youth talent development and resilience of micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs).
2. Post-maternity leave allowance (EPCB)
- The allowance provides financial assistance for up to 30 days of additional leave taken after the completion of the 98-day maternity leave period.
- It is expected to benefit more than 132,000 female workers.
3. Proposal to expand Socso coverage to Malaysians working abroad.
- Expected to benefit more than 480,000 workers.
- The government will strengthen the existing legal framework, including amendments to the Self-Employment Social Security Act 2017, to enable coverage to be extended to Malaysians working overseas, starting with cross-border workers between Malaysia and Singapore.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: "Peace has become a dirty word" as activists rally in Tel Aviv for two-state solution
Article:
TEL AVIV: Colourful images of doves decorated the backdrop of the stage where the Jerusalem Youth Choir singing in Hebrew and Arabic, opening the main event of the "People's Peace Summit."
The third edition of the annual event was held in Tel Aviv on Thursday and attended by hundreds of participants.
The conference was organised by the "It's Time" coalition, more than 80 organisations working to bring the Israeli-Palestinian conflict to an end with an agreement guaranteeing both peoples' right to self-determination.
The mood at the convention was one of stubborn hope.
Participants acknowledged that, as the country approaches general elections in October, left-wing activism under one of the most right-wing coalitions in its country's history has become increasingly challenging.
As one speaker said on stage: "Peace has become a dirty word."
"There is always a reason for optimism. And there is always a good reason to work for peace," Achinoam Nini told AFP.
Nini, 56, one of Israel's best-known singers internationally and a prominent face of the country's peace movement, performs under the stage name Noa.
With Israel still waging war on multiple fronts, she said, there is also a battle inside the country.
"We're fighting for it to be a liberal democracy ... Towards the elections, it's just going to get worse and worse," she said.
Although the conference was planned as a joint Israeli-Palestinian event, the bulk of attendees appeared to be veterans of Israel's peace camp, many of them acquainted through decades of shared activism.
Organisations including Peace Now, Standing Together and the Bereaved Families Forum set up stands in the main hall, promoting their work and selling merchandise.
Activists from the West Bank and Gaza were unable to attend, blocked by a permit system that has become near-impenetrable since the Hamas attacks of Oct  7, 2023 and the subsequent war in Gaza.
The event did, however, draw several dozen members of youth movements, as well as Palestinian citizens of Israel.
"If we don't unite all the peace movements, the situation will be difficult in this country. We are trying to be an influential part of the government," said Tahani Abd al-Halim, 44, a teacher from Kafr Manda in northern Israel.
"There must be change. Two peoples must live together. And the government must be not only the Jewish people but also the Arab people," she said.
During one of the panels preceding the main event, a video message from French foreign minister Jean-Noel Barrot announced that France will host an international meeting in June dedicated to a two-state solution to the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, one year after a previous such conference.
Israeli media coverage of the Tel Aviv event was conspicuously thin – a reflection of how marginal the peace camp has become in the national conversation.
Also absent were representatives of the mainstream centrist parties in the Knesset, Israel's parliament, let alone those of the right.
A handful of left-wing and Arab lawmakers did attend, among them Ofer Cassif, 61, of the far-left Hadash party.
"I'm expecting the worst but hoping for the best," the politician told AFP.
"What we have been trying to do is to pursue our beliefs and to do whatever we can in order to materialise a better future for both peoples of this country, of this land, and the whole region," he added.
"And I believe that eventually we will prevail. The question is when."--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Japan extends Petronas urea deal amid supply fears
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Japan will extend its urea procurement contract with national oil company Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) amid concerns over potential disruptions to global fertiliser supply due to ongoing tensions in the Middle East.
According to a news report by Japan's public broadcaster NHK, the agreement comes as the country seeks to secure a stable supply of urea, a key raw material used in fertiliser production.
Japan's agriculture, forestry and fisheries minister Norikazu Suzuki said the country currently sources about three-quarters of its urea imports from Malaysia.
"Amid the continuing uncertainty in the Middle East, producers will need fertilisers in autumn and I believe there are also concerns about next year," he said in the report.
Japan had previously entered into a two-year agreement to import urea through a Petronas subsidiary.
The extension is seen as a precautionary move to safeguard supply continuity.
Data from Suzuki's ministry showed that Malaysia accounted for 74 per cent of Japan's 256,000 tonnes of urea imports between June 2024 and June 2025, followed by Vietnam at 10 per cent.
Japan's Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries Ministry reportedly, in a separate statement,  said Suzuki travelled to Bangladesh and Malaysia from April 28 to May 1 to hold meetings with government officials.
He also went on site visits to strengthen bilateral relations and expand exports of Japanese agricultural, forestry and fishery products, and food.
On Friday, Suzuki exchanged views with Malaysian companies supplying Japan with urea, a raw material for fertilisers, as well as crude oil and petroleum products.
He also held discussions with local retailers in Malaysia to expand exports of Japanese agricultural, forestry, and fishery products in addition to food.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Yayasan Peneraju taps 2,000 Bumiputera youths for accounting pathways
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Yayasan Peneraju has expanded its nationwide outreach to groom future accountants, reaching more than 2,000 Bumiputera youths through its 2026 '"Jom Explore! Why Not YP?" programme.
The initiative, now in its second year, is designed to guide SPM leavers towards internationally recognised professional accounting qualifications via a faster and more structured pathway.
Yayasan Peneraju said eligible participants were introduced to accelerated pathways to pursue professional certifications such as the Association of Chartered Certified Accountants and the Institute of Chartered Accountants in England and Wales.
Successful applicants can receive up to RM150,000 in financial support, covering tuition fees, examination costs and study materials, alongside a monthly allowance of about RM1,000 throughout their studies.
Yayasan Peneraju chief executive officer Ibrahim Sani said the agency remains committed to developing high-potential individuals into value creators who contribute meaningfully to the nation's development.
"Through the programme, we have been able to reach Bumiputera youth nationwide at an important point in their educational journey, particularly as many had just received their SPM results.
"The programme was designed to provide a clear and well-supported pathway that can help school leavers progress towards becoming chartered accountants faster than through the conventional route," said Ibrahim.
The 2026 edition began with Wave 1 here from February to March 2, followed by Wave 2 from April 4-6, before making its way to Kuching, Sarawak on April 13 and Kota Kinabalu, Sabah on April 18.
Training placements are offered at established institutions including Sunway College, INTEC Education College, UOW Malaysia KDU College and Tymba Education Group.
Graduates may achieve affiliate membership status as early as age 21, with starting salaries of around RM4,000 and potential earnings of RM8,000 or more within three years of qualification.
To date, more than 9,000 Bumiputera talents nationwide have benefited from YP's professional accounting programmes resulting from its outreach efforts.
Yayasan Peneraju aims to support the development of 2,000 more professionally qualified accountants in 2026, with a longer-term ambition of producing 5,000 chartered accountants annually by 2030.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid: Entertainment must respect values, sensitivities
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said that any public programme must respect religious sensitivities, cultural values and local norms.
He said organisers must ensure their events do not disregard these principles, adding that such concerns reflect the sentiments of a significant portion of Malaysians.
"In any programme, the principles must be clear. It cannot sideline values, etiquette, religious sensitivities and local societal norms," he said in a statement.
The deputy prime minister also said the government takes seriously the views raised by the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Department and the Federal Territories Mufti Department, describing them as reflective of public concerns.
"As such, we have asked for programmes of this nature to be reviewed in terms of concept, content and implementation.
"Guidelines must also be refined and tightened to ensure every event aligns with the nation's cultural, religious and identity values."
He added that while entertainment is not wrong, it must be exercised within limits.
"Our principle is clear: entertainment is permissible, but there must be boundaries. Progress can continue, but values must not be compromised," he said, adding that organisers should engage with authorities to preserve social harmony.
Zahid said this reflects Umno's stance on the matter.
His remarks come amid ongoing public discourse over entertainment events and their alignment with community values.
In Kuantan, the local council recently rejected applications to hold water-themed music festivals at
Teluk Cempedak and Cherating,
citing religious, moral and cultural sensitivities.
Meanwhile in Kuala Lumpur, the
Rain Rave Water Music Festival
is ongoing in Bukit Bintang, drawing both support for its tourism potential and criticism from some quarters over its concept.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang extends quit rent appeal period to May 31
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The Penang state government has extended the appeal period for cases arising from the implementation of the revised quit rent (land tax) until May 31, said Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow.
Chow said the decision was made at the State Executive Council meeting on April 29, which agreed to extend the appeal period for cases linked to the revised land tax rates.
"I once again call on landowners to submit all appeals and feedback through official channels, namely the nearest district offices or the Land and Mines Office, for any concerns, including confirmation of current land use for further review.
"In the meantime, the State Executive Council has also agreed to grant an exemption on late payment charges to all landowners who settle their taxes after May 31. The exemption will cover late fees for land tax, parcel tax and temporary block tax," he said in a statement today.
He said the state government hoped the exemption would give landowners the opportunity to settle their taxes promptly and avoid arrears in their tax accounts by 2027.
Chow said the approach was aimed at helping landowners adjust to the revised land tax rates, which have not been reviewed for more than 30 years, while also easing the financial burden during the transition period.
He added that as of April 30, a total of 10,344 responses or appeals had been received through official forms submitted to district offices statewide.
"Of the total, 4,015 cases have been resolved, while 6,329 are still being processed by the district and land offices as well as the Penang State Land and Mines Director's Office," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: CelcomDigi restores data services after upgrade disruption
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: CelcomDigi, the country's largest mobile service operator, experienced an interruption to data services due to a systems upgrade yesterday.
The telecommunications giant said it had fully restored mobile data services for affected subscribers.
"We confirm that some CelcomDigi customers experienced a brief interruption to data services last evening.
"This occurred during a planned systems upgrade, which was communicated beforehand.
"Voice services remained operational throughout the exercise. All services have been restored," it said in a statement today.
Following the network recovery, CelcomDigi advised affected users to refresh their connection settings to ensure optimal performance.
"We advise customers to restart their mobile phones or toggle aeroplane mode on and off," the company added.
Formed on Nov 30, 2022, through the merger of Celcom and Digi, the company serves over 20 million users nationwide.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Disabled man in despair as RM5k loan from 'ah long' turns into debt nightmare
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A disabled man who borrowed RM5,000 from illegal moneylenders to settle a debt now faces a staggering 280 per cent interest rate, causing his repayments to skyrocket.
The victim, identified only as Ashraf, 29, said his troubles began when he sought funds to repay a RM2,400 debt to a friend.
He said he was contacted via WhatsApp by a person offering loans and later met an agent at a shopping mall in Subang Jaya to finalise the deal.
"I agreed to the loan because I was desperate to settle my debt with a friend who needed the money for his wedding.
"However, I was not informed that the loan was from an unlicensed moneylender," he said at a press conference held by the Malaysia International Humanitarian Organisation (MHO) today.
Ashraf, who suffers from seizures, said that although he applied for RM5,000, he only received RM3,800 after RM1,200 was deducted for processing fees.
He subsequently paid RM700 monthly for seven months, totalling RM4,900.
"Despite these payments, the balance still stands at RM12,500 under a 28-month contract. This burden made me doubt the legitimacy of the loan," he said.
Ashraf, who recently returned to work as a store assistant after being unemployed for two years following head surgery in April last year, lodged a police report on April 9.
He claimed the investigating officer scolded him for taking the loan.
"It has been 23 days since I filed the report, and I have yet to receive any feedback or updates on the investigation," he said.
MHO secretary-general Datuk Hishamuddin Hashim said the case should be investigated under Section 5(2) of the Moneylenders Act as the individuals involved had offered loans without a permit.
They advertised and provided loans without a licence while charging exorbitant interest rates.
"Opening an investigation paper is crucial to ensure action is taken and to give victims confidence that their cases are being handled," he said.
The New Straits Times is seeking comment from the police.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thunderstorm alert issued across multiple states until 4pm
Article:
MET0105
Thunderstorm alert issued across multiple states until 4pm
Caption
MetMalaysia warns of heavy rain and strong winds in short-term advisory. – Source: Malaysian Meteorological Dept
New Straits Times
KUALA LUMPUR: The Meteorological Department has issued a thunderstorm warning for several states, with heavy rain and strong winds expected until 4pm today.
The alert, issued at 1.15pm, covers parts of Kedah, Perak, Terengganu, Pahang, Selangor, Negri Sembilan, Melaka and Johor, as well as the Federal Territories of Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya.
In Sabah, the warning affects areas in the Interior, West Coast and Tawau divisions, including Sipitang, Tenom, Nabawan, Papar, Kunak, Semporna and Lahad Datu.
Among the districts listed are Kulim and Bandar Baharu (Kedah); Kerian, Kuala Kangsar and Kinta (Perak); Kuala Terengganu, Marang, and Kemaman (Terengganu); Cameron Highlands, Jerantut, and Raub (Pahang); as well as Gombak, Petaling and Sepang in Selangor.
MetMalaysia said such warnings are issued when there are signs of thunderstorms producing rainfall exceeding 20mm per hour, or when such conditions are expected to persist for more than an hour.
It added that thunderstorm warnings are short-term advisories, valid for no more than six hours for each issuance.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UK police charge man with stabbing attack on two Jewish Londoners
Article:
LONDON: UK police said on Friday they had charged a 45-year-old man over the stabbing of two Jewish men in north London, the latest in a string of hate attacks.
"Essa Suleiman... has been charged with two counts of attempted murder and one count of possession of a bladed article in a public place in relation to an attack in Golders Green on Wednesday," the Metropolitan Police said in a statement.
UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer vowed Thursday to boost security for the Jewish community after accusations from angry British Jews that his government has repeatedly failed to protect them.
Later Thursday, the interior ministry announced that the country's terrorism threat level had been raised to "severe", the second highest in the five-tier system and meaning another attack "is highly likely in the next six months."
The two men were attacked in broad daylight in Golders Green, a north London area with a large Jewish population.
The victims, aged 76 and 34, were in a stable condition in hospital.
The suspected attacker is a British national who was born in Somalia and came to the UK as a child. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Foreigners begging at KLIA arrested after videos go viral
Article:
SEPANG: Two foreign men were arrested at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) today for allegedly begging for money to cover their daily expenses.
The suspects, a 28-year-old Tajikistani and a 45-year-old Palestinian, are United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) cardholders.
Klia police chief Assistant Commissioner M. Ravi said the arrests followed a complaint from Malaysia Airports Aviation Security (Avsec).
An Avsec patrol team observed the duo approaching members of the public to ask for money at 2am.
They were identified as the same people seen in viral social media videos involving begging activities at the airport.
The suspects were taken to the airport's main terminal police station for further investigation.
"They were seen behaving suspiciously at a fast food restaurant at the airport's observation deck, where they allegedly solicited money.
"Both suspects admitted they did not possess valid passports or travel documents to remain in Malaysia, and are UNHCR cardholders.
"They also admitted to coming to KLIA to solicit donations from the public by appealing to sympathy and citing hardship," he said.
The Palestinian suspect claimed he did not know the other man. Police records show he has prior cases linked to theft in Kedah, as well as voluntarily causing hurt and criminal intimidation in Seri Kembangan.
A 14-day remand order was obtained today to facilitate investigations under Section 6(1)(c) of the Immigration Act for residing in the country without a valid permit.
Offenders can be jailed for up to five years, fined up to RM10,000, caned, or face a combination of these penalties.
The suspects have been referred to the Narcotics Investigation Department for urine screening. Their UNHCR cards have been seized for verification.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Malaysia working to free detained Global Sumud activists
Article:
RAWANG: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia is working with other countries to secure the release of Global Sumud Flotilla activists detained by Israel.
Anwar said he had been in touch with the Foreign Ministry and would continue contacting counterparts in other countries to help secure their release.
"We are now taking action by contacting our friends, the Gulf states and Arab countries, to find a way to free them from Israeli detention.
"I have been in contact with the Foreign Ministry and we are contacting friendly countries. Whoever I can contact, I will contact," he told reporters after Friday prayers at Masjid Nurul Iman here today.
Anwar said many of the activists were detained in international waters, describing Israel's actions as a "mad act" that disregards international principles.
"This is not a case of them entering or trespassing into Israeli waters.
"They were still in international waters," he said, adding that some activists had been forced to move into Greek waters, where their safety was guaranteed by the Greek government.
Anwar said the detained activists, who include 10 Malaysians, were carrying food, water, medicine and clothing, not weapons.
He said Israel's action showed continued oppression against those trying to deliver humanitarian aid to Palestinians in Gaza.
"They do not care about the killing of women and children; they do not care about attacks on Iran, Lebanon and Gaza, and now those who want to help," Anwar said.
Malaysia supports the establishment of an independent Palestinian state and does not maintain diplomatic relations with Israel.
It has consistently voiced support for Palestinian rights and called for an end to the conflict in Gaza through international forums and diplomatic channels.
Ten Malaysian humanitarian activists were among 186 individuals
detained or reported as uncontactable
following the interception of the Global Sumud Flotilla 2.0 by Israeli forces.
The interception took place in international waters near Crete, Greece.
The Malaysians were Zainal Rashid Ahmad, Osman Zolkifli, Dr Jihan Alya Mohd Nordin, Mohd Shamsir Mohd Isa, Hazwan Hazim Dermawan, Mohd Hanafi Mohd Salim, Ahmad Musa Al-Nuwayri Kamaruzaman, Norhelmi Ab Ghani, Mohd Redzal Amzah and Muhamad Muhsin Zaidi.
Anwar yesterday
denounced
the Israeli regime as "cowardly and insane" for detaining the activists.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Blossom Wong, ex-Special Branch and undercover operative, dies
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Blossom Wong, one of Malaysia's most well-known Special Branch officers and undercover operatives, has died.
She was 87.
Wong had been receiving treatment at Kuala Lumpur Hospital after suffering a stroke. Her death was confirmed by her daughter, Dr Christina Blossom Welch who recieved the news around 9am.
Wong, according to reports, was a quiet but formidable figure in the police force.
As a Special Branch officer, she worked undercover during the communist insurgency era, and later served in the Criminal Investigation Department (CID), where she was involved in anti-vice operations in Kuala Lumpur.
She subsequently served in CID's "Black Cats" unit, tasked with dismantling prostitution and vice syndicates operating in the capital.
To her family, however, she was simply a mother who carried the weight of an extraordinary career with quiet discipline at home.
Dr Christina said her mother's time in the force never weakened the bond she had with her children.
"She taught us how to be independent," she told the New Straits Times.
"We never felt like she was not there. We understood."
She described her mother as strict, particularly during her early upbringing, shaped by her role as a single parent.
"She was very strict… but it was because she wanted me to stand on my own two feet," she said.
Dr Christina added that her mother was uncompromising in her values.
"Yes is yes, no is no. There's no cheating, no in-between," she said. "What you say you will do, you must do."
According to a 2025 Rojak Daily article, Wong joined the police force in the 1950s, at a time when women in uniform were still rare.
She was formally inducted in 1957 and went on to serve in the Special Branch, working undercover in sensitive internal security operations.
[[nid:1430366]]
Dr Christina said Wong rarely spoke about her assignments.
"There were many cases… just too many," she said. "Not one in particular."
Her duties often required long absences and strict confidentiality, leaving much of her professional life unspoken within the household. Still, Dr Christina said there was never resentment in the family.
"She would be there if something came up, but otherwise she taught us to manage on our own."
Despite her strictness, Dr Christina said Wong became more gentle in later years.
"Later on, she became more 'manja'… she would do things for me," she said.
Wong was later transferred to CID, where she became part of the "Black Cats" unit, known for dismantling prostitution and vice syndicates operating in Kuala Lumpur.
Reflecting on her mother's career, Dr Christina said she was deeply affected by her work in protecting vulnerable women and girls.
"She felt she had saved so many young girls," she said. "She (often) told herself — I have a daughter, so how can I allow this to happen?"
[[nid:1430365]]
Wong retired in 1993 after more than three decades of service, ending her career as a superintendent of police.
While she was known within law enforcement circles for her role in high-risk operations and escort duties involving visiting foreign dignitaries, those closest to her remember a quieter reality — a life defined by duty, restraint and sacrifice.
A wake will be held at Nirvana 2 on Sunday (May 3) and Monday (May 4). Wong will be laid to rest on Tuesday (May 5).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran activates air defences as Trump faces congressional deadline
Article:
TEHERAN: Teheran's air defences were activated to counter small aircraft and drones late Thursday, as the White House signalled that it will not be reined in by a congressional deadline on the Iran war.
The Tasnim and Fars news agencies reported that air defence systems, heard in some parts of the Iranian capital, were activated "to counter small aircraft and reconnaissance drones" for around 20 minutes but that the situation had returned to "normal."
US President Donald Trump's administration faced a looming midnight deadline to secure congressional authorisation for the war against Iran, setting up a clash between the White House and Congress.
The Trump administration argued that the 60-day clock to seek authorisation was effectively paused by a ceasefire announced last month.
"For War Powers Resolution purposes, the hostilities that began on Saturday, February 28 have terminated," a senior administration official told AFP late on Thursday, noting that there has been no exchange of fire between the United States and Iran since the April 7 ceasefire.
'SHAMEFUL DEFEAT'
Earlier on Thursday, Iran's supreme leader declared that the United States had suffered a shameful defeat, defiantly rejecting a warning from Trump that an economically punishing US naval blockade could be enforced for months to come.
Oil prices hit a four-year high, then fell back slightly before Mojtaba Khamenei issued a written statement read on state television declaring that Iran was now in the driver's seat in the crisis.
"Today, two months after the largest military deployment and aggression by the world's bullies in the region, and the United States' disgraceful defeat in its plans, a new chapter is unfolding for the Persian Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz," he said, hailing Iran's control over shipping in the strait.
He went on to predict a bright future for the Gulf without the United States, saying those who interfere in the region from afar "have no place there except at the bottom of its waters."
Khamenei was wounded in the initial US-Israeli strikes that killed his father, Ali Khamenei, and has not been seen in public since being named his successor as supreme leader in March.
The United States imposed a blockade on Iran's ports two weeks ago, while the Islamic republic has maintained its stranglehold over the strategic Strait of Hormuz since the start of the Middle East war at the end of February.
Washington is now seeking to assemble an international coalition of allied states and shipping firms to coordinate safe passage through Hormuz – while maintaining its blockade of ships serving Iran, a State Department official told AFP.
Trump threatened Thursday to withdraw US troops from Italy and Spain, extending similar warnings already made against Germany, after lambasting the NATO allies for failing to support US-Israeli operations against Iran, including in the Strait.
'ACT AGAIN'
Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz warned meanwhile that it was "possible that we may soon have to act again" against Iran to achieve the war's objectives.
But the commander of the Iranian Revolutionary Guards aerospace force, Majid Mousavi, said even a "short and tactical" enemy operation would be met "with painful, prolonged, and extensive strikes."
Tehran residents speaking earlier to AFP journalists in Paris described a sense of despair that the Islamic republic government was clinging to power and that negotiations had stalled.
"From the Islamic Republic still being in place to the innocent people whose lives were destroyed in this war, everything is so disappointing," one 28-year-old IT worker told AFP via messaging app from the Iranian capital.
'INTOLERABLE'
Trump has reportedly told oil executives and national security officials this week to prepare for a prolonged US blockade designed to force Tehran to surrender its nuclear programme.
US Central Command said Wednesday it had redirected a total of 44 commercial vessels to violate the blockade as part of its blockade of Iran.
Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said the blockade of his country's ports was effectively an "extension of military operations" by Washington, despite the ongoing ceasefire.
"Continuation of this oppressive approach is intolerable," he added.
Oil prices struck a four-year high Thursday. International benchmark Brent crude soared more than seven percent to $126 a barrel before easing in afternoon trading in London.
UN chief Antonio Guterres said the closure of Hormuz was "strangling the global economy," and International Energy Agency chief Fatih Birol told a meeting at his Paris headquarters: "The world is facing the biggest energy crisis in history."
"The consequences of the Middle East crisis grow dramatically worse with each passing hour... Now is the time for dialogue, for solutions that pull us back from the brink," Guterres wrote on X on Friday.
US URGES ISRAEL-LEBANON TALKS
Violence has continued on the war's Lebanese front, with the US embassy in Lebanon on Thursday urging a meeting between Lebanese and Israeli leaders.
Israeli and Lebanese representatives have met twice in Washington in recent weeks – the first such meetings in decades – after the Iran-backed Hezbollah group drew Lebanon into the Middle East war on March 2.
Trump has said he hopes to host Lebanese President Joseph Aoun and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu "over the next couple of weeks."
Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon killed at least 15 people, the Lebanese health ministry said Thursday, while the Lebanese army said a separate strike on a home in the south killed a soldier and multiple members of his family. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar urges calm in Negri Sembilan political crisis
Article:
RAWANG: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged all parties in Negri Sembilan to allow the state's political situation to cool down, saying stability is crucial to avoid disrupting efforts to restore the economy and safeguard public interests amid global uncertainties.
He said the matter must be handled in accordance with the state constitution and relevant laws.
"Negri Sembilan, let the situation cool down first. As we know, there are issues that must be addressed according to the state constitution.
"In the meantime, we follow the decree of the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, who has asked the menteri besar to continue administering the state," he told reporters after joining Friday prayers at Masjid Nurul Iman here today.
Anwar said any resolution should be pursued through proper legal and constitutional channels, while urging political leaders not to allow the situation to distract from governance priorities.
"We will negotiate, but for now, do not disrupt efforts to restore the economy and protect the people's interests. That is important," he said.
His remarks come amid recent political developments in the state, where all 14 Barisan Nasional (BN) assemblymen in Negri Sembilan agreed to continue supporting the unity government following a meeting with Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
Zahid, who is also Deputy Prime Minister, said the decision was reached after discussions with the assemblymen, following earlier controversy over their reported withdrawal of support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
Anwar had previously stated that Aminuddin would continue serving as menteri besar following a decree by Yang di-Pertuan Besar Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, adding that the situation was being addressed through legal and constitutional processes.
On April 27, Negri Sembilan Umno and BN had withdrawn support for the state government, citing issues linked to the state's Adat Perpatih customary system and its interaction with the constitutional framework.
Negri Sembilan Umno chief Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias had said the party leadership took note of developments involving the Adat Perpatih system and its constitutional interpretation, adding that BN executive councillors were not initially briefed on discussions at the state level before the matter escalated into public debate.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang schools battered by storm, officials pledge swift repairs and safety for students
Article:
KEPALA BATAS, May 1 — Four schools in Seberang Perai Utara (SPU), including a primary school, were damaged by a storm late yesterday evening.
Penang Education director Mohamad Dziauddin Mat Saad said the schools involved were Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Datuk Haji Abdul Kadir and Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Hashim Awang in Kepala Batas, as well as SMK Gemilang and SMK Permatang Tok Jaya in Butterworth.
He said the worst-hit school was SMK Datuk Haji Abdul Kadir, where the roof of a block housing five classrooms was torn off by strong winds, causing structural damage to the first-floor section of the building.
Meanwhile, the other schools sustained only minor damage involving certain rooms, which will not affect teaching sessions as repair works can be carried out soon.
“What is most important is the students’ safety, and thankfully, the incident occurred after school hours.
“Over the next three days until Sunday, we will focus on clean-up works to ensure that students returning to the hostel on Sunday are safe and that schooling can proceed smoothly on Monday,” he told reporters here today.
Earlier, he conducted an inspection at SMK Haji Abdul Kadir to assess the damage, together with Bertam state assemblyman Datuk Seri Reezal Merican Naina Merican.
Mohamad Dziauddin said a discussion had been held with the school, and the principal had identified several rooms, such as laboratories and workshops, to be used as temporary classrooms for the five affected classes involving about 230 form two students.
He added that the school would also brief students upon their return on Monday before arranging their placement in the temporary classes.
Meanwhile, Reezal Merican said the storm that hit Kepala Batas yesterday caused various damages to private premises as well as educational institutions, and initial assistance had been arranged for those affected.
He said it is estimated that more than 90 houses, including business premises, schools, a hospital and a fire station in Kepala Batas, were affected by the storm and strong winds that struck at around 6pm yesterday, with most of the damage involving roofs being blown off. — Bernama

Title: Defence minister defends high‑achieving SPM student, calls for end to online criticism
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, May 1 — Kota Tinggi Member of Parliament Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin has called on the public to cease all forms of criticism and speculation against a high-achieving Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) student, following the circulation of a video on social media recently.
He said the footage showed a casual interaction between him and students during an event in the Kota Tinggi parliamentary and Sedili state constituencies honouring prospective haj pilgrims and 2025 SPM high-achieving students.
According to him, the interaction was misinterpreted by certain parties, leading to various negative reactions, including criticism directed at the student.
“In this regard, I would like to stress that there is no issue arising from the interaction.
“The student’s response should be seen as a spontaneous reaction, possibly influenced by nervousness when facing an MP and a public audience,” he said in a statement.
In this regard, he stressed that all parties must exercise greater discretion in their reactions, especially when it involves students, to avoid negative impacts on their emotional well-being.
Mohamed Khaled, who is also the Defence Minister, said the achievements of students who obtained excellent SPM results should be celebrated.
Meanwhile, he also emphasised the importance of continued commitment from all parties in supporting the character development and excellence of the younger generation.
“Every programme involving students should be conducted in a positive and constructive environment,” he said.
Recently, a video showing the interaction between Mohamed Khaled and the high-achieving student went viral on Threads, drawing criticism from some quarters.
In the video, the female student was seen replying ‘mahu jadi orang’ (I just want to be someone) while laughing when asked about her ambitions for the future, which drew mixed reactions from netizens, with some describing the response as impolite. — Bernama

Title: Safety of 175 Gaza flotilla activists, including 10 Malaysians, still unconfirmed 38 hours after Israeli detention
Article:
BANGI, May 1 — The Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) has yet to receive any official confirmation on the safety status of 175 Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 activists, including 10 Malaysian participants, after being detained by Israeli authorities in international waters 38 hours ago.
SNCC director-general Datuk Dr Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said while there was initial information that all the activists would be released at Lerapetra Port, Crete, Greece at noon today (Malaysia time), it has yet to be verified.
“The current status (of the information) has yet to be confirmed through official channels and our field group at the port,” he said at a special media conference on updates on the Global Sumud Flotilla 2026 here today.
Sani Araby said they have also been in contact with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim who has stated his firm stand and condemned the Israeli regime for violating international laws.
“(The Prime Minister) stated his continued commitment to ensure the release of the participants,” he added.
Sani Araby said the Prime Minister informed that the government through Wisma Putra has begun diplomatic efforts by contacting the Malaysian honorary consul in Greece and other friendly countries to assist and coordinate efforts to release the activists.
He also urged Malaysians to continue to pressure and state their stand on the violation of international laws by the Zionist regime in Gaza and Palestine.
“SNCC has been informed that efforts are being made with legal groups around the world to apply continued pressure on the Israeli regime through respective diplomatic efforts to release the participants.
“We urge all Malaysians to pray and do whatever they can in sympathy for those being detained and also outrage against the violations committed by the Israeli regime,” he said, adding that all MPs, backbenchers and Opposition, should unite to pressure the regime over their actions in detaining the activists in international waters that was a violation of human rights.
He reiterated that the regime’s actions would not deter other activists and the delegations in Greece and Turkiye were determined to carry on with their voyage.
The ten Malaysians detained yesterday are Zainal Rashid Ahmad onboard Arkham III; Osman Zolkifli and Dr Jihan Alya Mohd Nordin (Eros 1); Hazwan Hazim Dermawan, Mohd Hanafi Mohd Salim, Ahmad Musa Al-Nuwayri Kamaruzaman and Norhelmi Ab Ghani (Bianca BCN), Mohd Redzal Amzah (Freia); Muhamad Muhsin Zaidi (Marea) and Mohd Shamsir Mohd Isa (Esplai). — Bernama

Title: Prime Minister Anwar calls for restraint, stresses economy over politics in Negeri Sembilan crisis
Article:
RAWANG, May 1 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged all parties to remain calm in addressing the situation in Negeri Sembilan and to prioritise efforts to revive the economy and safeguard the interests of the people.
He said the situation in the state must be handled in accordance with the provisions of the state constitution, while respecting the decree of the Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun to continue in office.
Anwar stressed that the priority now is to restore the national economy rather than to escalate political issues.
“We have given our advice, and I have conveyed this to our friends in Pakatan Harapan, and also to the leadership of Umno.
“If discussions are needed, we can have them, but for now, we must not disrupt efforts to revive the economy and safeguard the interests of the people. That is what matters most,” he told reporters after performing Friday prayers at Masjid Nurul Iman here today.
On Monday, all 14 Umno and Barisan Nasional assemblymen in Negeri Sembilan announced they had lost confidence in and withdrawn support from the state government led by Aminuddin. — Bernama

Title: Foreign woman dies after falling from 13th‑floor apartment during police narcotics raid in Nilai
Article:
NILAI, May 1 — A foreign woman in her 20s died after she was believed to have fallen from the 13th floor of an apartment building in Bandar Baru Nilai here at 1.51am today.
Negeri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the Nilai district police headquarters confirmed receiving information about the death that happened while a police team was conducting a narcotics raid at the apartment building.
“During the raid, the woman was believed to have tried to escape through the balcony of the apartment unit before slipping and falling from the 13th floor,” he said in a statement here today.
A motorcycle patrol unit was sent to the location and found the victim in an unconscious state, he said, adding that a medical team from the Nilai health clinic pronounced her dead at the scene and sent the body to Rembau Hospital for an autopsy.
“Preliminary investigations at the scene did not reveal any criminal elements so far and the case is classified as sudden death, but further investigations are ongoing to secure witness statements,” he said.
He advised the public to not speculate on the matter as it could disrupt police investigations and urged those with information on the death to assist investigations by contacting the Nilai district police headquarters or the nearest police station. — Bernama

Title: Heavy rain and strong winds forecast nationwide, MetMalaysia issues sweeping alert until evening
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit nearly all states across the country until 6.00pm this evening.
The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) issued an alert at 3.50 pm, stating that the affected states in the northern region are Perlis and Kedah, covering the districts of Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Yan, Pendang and Sik.
In Perak, the areas involved are Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim.
Similar conditions are also forecast for several areas on the east coast, in Kelantan (Jeli, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang), Terengganu (Besut, Setiu, Hulu Terengganu, Marang, Dungun and Kemaman), as well as Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Jerantut, Bera and Rompin).
The same warning has also been issued for Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak and Petaling in Selangor, as well as the entire Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur.
In the southern region, the warning involves Negeri Sembilan (Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah, Rembau, Jempol and Tampin), the entire state of Melaka, and Johor (Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Batu Pahat, Kluang, Pontian, Kulai and Johor Bahru).
Meanwhile, in Sabah, the forecast covers the Interior (Sipitang, Tenom and Nabawan), West Coast (Papar), and Tawau (Kunak, Semporna, and Lahad Datu).
In Sarawak, the affected areas are Kuching (Bau and Lundu), Sarikei (Julau and Meradong), Sibu, Mukah Kapit (Song), Bintulu (Bintulu and Sebauh), Miri (Subis, Beluru, Telang Usan and Marudi) and Limbang. — Bernama

Title: Blossom Wong, feared Special Branch undercover officer, dies at 87
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Blossom Wong, one of Malaysia’s best-known Special Branch officers and undercover operatives, has died at the age of 87,
New Straits Times
reported.
Wong had been receiving treatment at Kuala Lumpur Hospital after suffering a stroke. Her death was confirmed by her daughter, Dr Christina Blossom Welch, who said she received the news around 9am.
According to
New Straits Times
, Wong was regarded as a quiet but formidable figure in the police force.
As a Special Branch officer, she worked undercover during the communist insurgency era before later serving in the Criminal Investigation Department (CID), where she was involved in anti-vice operations in Kuala Lumpur.
She subsequently served in CID’s “Black Cats” unit, tasked with dismantling prostitution and vice syndicates operating in the capital.
To her family, she was a mother who carried an extraordinary career with quiet discipline at home.
Dr Christina told New Straits Times that her mother’s service in the force never affected their bond.
“She taught us how to be independent,” she said when contacted by
New Straits Times.
“We never felt like she was not there. We understood.”
She described Wong as strict during her early upbringing, shaped by her role as a single parent.
“She was very strict… but it was because she wanted me to stand on my own two feet,” she was quoted as saying.
Dr Christina added that her mother lived by firm values.
“Yes is yes, no is no. There’s no cheating, no in-between,” she said. “What you say you will do, you must do.”
Wong retired in 1993 after more than three decades of service, ending her career as a superintendent of police.
A wake will be held at Nirvana 2 on Sunday and Monday, with Wong to be laid to rest on Tuesday.

Title: Workers welcome post-maternity leave allowance, say it eases burden on new mothers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The introduction of the Post-Maternity Leave Allowance, announced by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, reflects the government’s concern for working mothers in addressing the rising cost of living.
Most public and private sector employees interviewed by Bernama said the additional one-month assistance would allow mothers who have just given birth to undergo their confinement period with greater peace of mind, without financial pressure.
Senior assistant director at the National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma), Nurfarehin Abd Aziz, 35, said the initiative reflects the government’s commitment to empowering women.
“It is very helpful, especially in the current economic situation, which is placing pressure on many people.
“At the very least, mothers who have just given birth can go through their confinement period more calmly without having to worry too much about financial matters,” she told Bernama after the 2026 National Workers’ Day celebration themed ‘Pekerja Madani Kesuma Bangsa’ at the Unifi Arena here today.
Senior administrative assistant at the Penang State Secretary’s Office, Laily Othman, 56, said the initiative would greatly assist female civil servants in planning their maternity leave.
“When we are given longer leave with allowance support, we can maintain our mental well-being and spend quality time with our families. This is a commendable effort to uplift women,” she said.
A nurse at the Sepang District Health Office, Nur Farah Diyana Hairo Azaman, 33, who has three children, said the allowance would benefit mothers, especially those who require a longer recovery period after childbirth.
“It provides room for better recovery while also enabling breastfeeding programmes to be carried out more effectively,” she said.
When delivering his message at the 2026 National Workers’ Day celebration, the Prime Minister announced that the government will introduce the Post-Maternity Leave Allowance as a progressive measure to support women’s participation in the workforce and to ease the pressures faced by mothers after childbirth.
He said the initiative was introduced following findings that the labour force participation rate among women, particularly those aged between 25 and 39, has shown a slight decline, partly due to maternity commitments.
